issues_found: FABRICATED HEDGING NUMBER AT L0=82. Section 5.2 claims strict hedging rises to 12.3% at L0=82. Raw data (hedging_decomposition_full.json, L0_82_to_176) shows strict_hedging=0, strict_hedging_pct=0.0%. The 12.3% figure appears in NO source data file. Pilot phase0 data shows 3.66% at L0=82 (3/82 FNs), also not 12.3%. This is a writing-generation hallucination that undermines trust in all numerical claims.

PROBE QUALITY CONFOUND UNRESOLVED -- DEGRADED-PROBE ABLATION NOT EXECUTED. The paper's cross-domain contribution (Kruskal-Wallis p=0.005) compares hierarchies at F1=0.97 (first-letter) vs F1=0.79-0.84 (RAVEL). Probe F1 correlates with FN rate at rho=-0.756. The confound is acknowledged (Section 4.3) but not experimentally resolved. The degraded-probe ablation (inject label noise into first-letter probes at F1={0.70, 0.80, 0.85, 0.90}, remeasure absorption) would definitively distinguish hierarchy effect from probe artifact. This is the single highest-impact missing experiment, identified by BOTH supervisor and critic as the top priority.

ACTIVATION PATCHING LIMITED TO L12 (5.7% ABSORPTION), NOT TESTED AT L24 (34.5% ABSORPTION). The causal evidence (32.5% recovery, d=1.33) is strong but comes from the layer with LOWEST relevant absorption (5.7%). The headline finding -- 34.5% absorption at L24 -- has NO causal validation. The paper proves causation where absorption is mild and only claims correlation where absorption is severe. This disconnects the causal evidence from the main empirical contribution.

LETTER G DOMINATES STRICT HEDGING (37.5% OF ALL CASES) WITHOUT INVESTIGATION. In the L0=22 decomposition (304 FNs), letter G accounts for 9/24 strict hedging cases (37.5%), with a per-letter strict rate of 64.3%. Letter A: 7/24 (29.2%). Together, G and A are 16/24 = 66.7% of all strict hedging. Excluding both, the strict rate drops to ~2.9% (8/272). The paper reports G as notable but does not compute statistics without it or investigate the mechanism.

TWO BROKEN CROSS-REFERENCES SIGNAL INCOMPLETE PREPARATION. (1) Section 8.2 cites 'Table 7 in Section 4.4 of the extended results' -- neither Table 7 nor Section 4.4 exists. (2) Section 3.5 references 'Section 8.6' for future patching work -- Future Directions is Section 8.5; no 8.6 exists. A reviewer encountering phantom table/section references will flag the manuscript as carelessly prepared.

TWO MISSING FIGURES LEAVE SECTIONS 5.1 AND 5.2 WITHOUT VISUAL SUPPORT. The outline planned 6 figures; only 4 exist. Figure 5 (activation patching paired dot plot showing per-word child vs control recovery) and Figure 6 (hedging decomposition stacked bar: 7.9%/86.2%/5.9%) are completely absent. Sections 5.1 and 5.2 -- containing two of the paper's four contributions -- are entirely text-heavy. Both figures can be generated from existing JSON data at zero GPU cost.

TITLE FEATURES A FAILED THEORETICAL FRAMEWORK. Title is 'The Absorption Tax: Layer-Dependent and Hierarchy-Dependent Feature Absorption Across Semantic Domains in Sparse Autoencoders.' The Absorption Tax is a theoretical framework that comprehensively FAILED (rho=-0.20, concordance 50%). It is relegated to Appendix D. Leading with 'The Absorption Tax' misleads the reader about the paper's actual contributions.

ARCHITECTURE COMPARISON UNDERPOWERED AND DOUBLY CONFOUNDED. N=16 at L12, RAVEL probes at F1=0.52-0.69 (worst layer), width mismatch (Matryoshka 32k vs JumpReLU/BatchTopK 16k), L0 mismatch (BatchTopK L0=20 vs JumpReLU L0=75-87). The p=0.87 null result has no statistical power. The claim 'hierarchy type explains more variance than architecture choice' compares L12 architecture test to L24 hierarchy test -- different layers, different confounds. This comparison is invalid.

ABSTRACT OVERCLAIMS RELATIVE TO EVIDENCE. The abstract presents cross-domain significance (p=0.005) and the probe quality confound (rho=-0.756) sequentially, giving the impression the confound is a minor caveat rather than an alternative explanation. The unconfounded finding (15x layer variation) should lead. Cross-domain variation should be explicitly marked 'suggestive'. Abstract is ~250 words, exceeding the NeurIPS limit.

CROSS-DOMAIN FINDING IS L24-ONLY; LAYER-HIERARCHY INTERACTION UNCHARACTERIZED. RAVEL probes measured only at L24. Pilot at L12 suggested the OPPOSITE pattern (semantic > first-letter). This crossover interaction means the cross-domain finding is layer-specific, not general. The paper claims both layer dependence and hierarchy dependence but cannot characterize their interaction.
issues_fixed: STAGNATION LOOP BROKEN: The 3-iteration stagnation (iter 6-7-8, all 6.5) is broken. Iter 9 executed experiments (activation patching, tightened hedging, CMI replication, threshold sensitivity) and produced a fully rewritten paper with new empirical content. Score rose from 6.5 to 7.0.

ACTIVATION PATCHING EXECUTED (EXP001 from iter_007 plan): 25 words tested, 19 with absorption, recovery 32.5% vs 1.5% (d=1.33, p=0.000218). Causal evidence for feature absorption established. 3 consecutive iterations of requesting this experiment finally executed.

TIGHTENED HEDGING EXECUTED (EXP002 from iter_007 plan): Multi-L0 analysis shows strict hedging 7.9%, compensatory 86.2%, persistent 5.9%. The near-tautological loose hedging critique resolved with concrete numbers. 3 consecutive iterations of requesting this experiment finally executed.

CMI REPLICATION AT L0=22 EXECUTED (EXP003 from iter_007 plan): rho=0.044, p=0.83. Definitive negative result. CMI correctly demoted to appendix. Resolves the overclaiming issue that persisted for 4 iterations.

THRESHOLD SENSITIVITY REPORTING EXECUTED (EXP004 from iter_007 plan): 5x4 grid analyzed, CV=0.077, FN constant at n=87. Absorption measurement confirmed threshold-robust. 3 consecutive iterations of requesting this finally completed.

CMI OVERCLAIMING RESOLVED: CMI demoted from main paper section to appendix with rho=0.044 definitive negative. The 4-iteration cycle of 'CMI predicts' overclaiming is broken by the definitive L0=22 negative result.

TWO-INTERPRETATION AMBIGUITY RESOLVED: Activation patching (d=1.33) confirms genuine competitive exclusion. The ambiguity between 'metric miscalibrated' and 'no absorption' is resolved in favor of real absorption.
